entries.definition:
sh–k–k to pierce, to puncture; to limp; to stick out; to doubt, to be confused, to be dubious, to waver in opinion; to attire oneself completely with arms and weapons. Of this root, شَكّ shakk occurs 15 times in the Qurʾan.
شَكّ shakk [n.] doubt, misgiving, suspicion (10:94) فَإِنْ كُنْتَ فِي شَكٍّ مِمَّا أَنْزَلْنَا إِلَيْكَ so if you [Prophet] are in doubt about what We have revealed to you, ask those who have been reading the Book before you.

شَكّ shakk [n.] doubt, misgiving, suspicion (10:94) فَإِنْ كُنْتَ فِي شَكٍّ مِمَّا أَنْزَلْنَا إِلَيْكَ so if you [Prophet] are in doubt about what We have revealed to you, ask those who have been reading the Book before you.
